comments:
Terminal Breakout — The playable demo proving the compositor thesis.

This module creates a complete Breakout game world:
- Paddle: controlled by arrow keys
- Ball: bounces off walls, paddle, and bricks
- Bricks: 5 rows × 10 columns, colored by row

## Terminal Dimensions

Terminal: 80 columns × 24 rows
Each character: 8×8 "pixels"
World: 640 × 192 pixels

## Entity Layout

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■   (row 0 - red)                                       │
│  ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■   (row 1 - orange)                                    │
│  ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■   (row 2 - yellow)                                    │
│  ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■   (row 3 - green)                                     │
│  ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■   (row 4 - blue)                                      │
│                                                                            │
│                          ●  (ball)                                         │
│                                                                            │
│                    ════════  (paddle)                                      │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

declarations.comments:
Create the complete Breakout world.

This is the main entry point for creating a new game.
Returns a World with:
- 1 paddle (entity 0)
- 1 ball (entity 1)
- 50 bricks (entities 2-51)

Demonstrates both composition styles:
- (#) pipe: world # addEntity paddle # addEntity ball
- ($) apply: addBricks $ addBall $ addPaddle $ world
declarations.title: breakout

declarations.comments:
Alternative construction using ($) style.
Equivalent to `breakout` but demonstrates right-to-left composition.
declarations.title: breakoutAlt

declarations.comments:
Get the color for a brick row.
Uses OKLCH for perceptually uniform colors:
- Row 0: Red (hue 0°)
- Row 1: Orange (hue 30°)
- Row 2: Yellow (hue 60°)
- Row 3: Green (hue 120°)
- Row 4: Blue (hue 240°)
declarations.title: rowColor

declarations.comments: Paddle color: bright white
declarations.title: paddleColor

declarations.comments: Ball color: bright yellow-orange
declarations.title: ballColor

declarations.comments:
Pixels per terminal character.

Used for coordinate conversion between world space and terminal cells.
Terminal cell (col, row) maps to world position (col * 8, row * 8).
declarations.title: pixelsPerChar

declarations.comments: Convert world X coordinate to terminal column.
declarations.title: worldToColumn

declarations.comments: Convert world Y coordinate to terminal row.
declarations.title: worldToRow

declarations.comments: Convert terminal column to world X coordinate.
declarations.title: columnToWorld

declarations.comments: Convert terminal row to world Y coordinate.
declarations.title: rowToWorld
